radish-with-butter-and-sea-salt-and-radi.name: Radish With Butter And Sea Salt And Radish Greens Salad
instructions: Pile radishes with pert green leaves still attached onto a plate. Set out a bowl of sea salt and a dish of butter. Pick up a radish, spread a bit of butter on it, sprinkle with salt, and eat the radish.
Place the leaves in a bowl. Drizzle the leaves with Dijon Vinaigrette, season to taste with salt and pepper, and enjoy as a salad.

radish-yogurt-with-pine-nuts.name: Radish Yogurt With Pine Nuts
instructions: Toss radishes in a small bowl with a couple pinches of salt. Let sit until salt begins to draw out water from radishes, about 5 minutes.
Meanwhile, toast pine nuts in a medium skillet over medium-high heat, tossing often, until golden brown, about 2 minutes. Transfer to a small bowl and let cool.
Massage radishes to release liquid, gently at first, then more vigorously as they start to expel water. Squeeze out excess liquid, then finely chop radishes (you should have about 1 cup).
Mix radishes, yogurt, lemon juice, 2 Tbsp. oil, and ½ tsp. lemon zest in a medium bowl; season with salt and pepper.
Divide yogurt among bowls, top with pine nuts and more lemon zest and drizzle with remaining 1 Tbsp. oil. Serve with cucumber spears for dipping.
Do Ahead: Yogurt can be made 3 days ahead; cover and chill. Top with pine nuts and lemon zest just before serving.

ramen-slaw.name: Ramen Slaw
instructions: Combine broccoli coleslaw mix, sunflower seeds, almonds, and ramen noodles in a bowl.
Whisk canola oil, sugar, vinegar, and ramen noodle seasoning packet together in a separate bowl; pour over slaw mixture. Fold in green onions. Chill in the refrigerator before serving, 1 ½ hours.

raspberry-mint-gt.name: Raspberry Mint G&t
instructions: To make raspberry syrup, add 1 cup of water, 1 cup of sugar, and 1 cup of raspberries to a pot.
Bring to a simmer for 3-5 minutes. Allow to cool. Strain off solids.
Gently muddle mint in glass with raspberry syrup and lime juice. Add Bombay Sapphire Gin and top with tonic water. Give a little stir to incorporate mint throughout the drink.

Garnish with raspberry pieces and mint sprigs.

raw-beet-salad.name: Raw Beet Salad
instructions: Stir grated beets, vinegar, oil, parsley, garlic, mustard, salt, and pepper together in a bowl. Serve immediately or for best results, refrigerate for 2 to 3 hours before serving.

recipe-for-breaded-fish-fingers-comedera.name: Recipe For Breaded Fish Fingers
recipe-for-breaded-fish-fingers-comedera.original_name: Receta de dedos de pescado rebozados
instructions: Corta los filetes de merluza en tiras alargadas, que tengan forma de dedos o bastones. Ponles una pizca de sal al gusto.

recipe-for-nutritious-eggplants-with-che.name: Recipe For Nutritious Eggplants With Cheese
recipe-for-nutritious-eggplants-with-che.original_name: Receta de berenjenas con queso nutritivas
instructions: Take eggplants and lasers and cut them in half. With the help of a knife, remove much of his pulp. It purifies to put salt both at the eggplant and pilling. That will prevent them from rustling and shattering their bitter taste.

Put a frying pan with olive oil and add onion and garlic to pieces or cut & quot; brunoise & quot;. Skip them till they are golden, add an eggplant's pulp to cook and sew with salt and pepper to taste. Cover and leave him for 5 minutes.

After time, add some of the tomato and oregano dough. Also, it incorporates a pint of sugar to lower the bitter taste of the tomate paste. Cook it till it's reduced and a compact mixture remains. When cooking, put them in a bowl to cool.

Enter them into the oven and leave them for 15 minutes. The idea is that everything gets rewarded.

While our eggplants and cheese are cooking. It heats up a frying pan, incorporates some olive oil, the rest of the tomate dough, oregano, salt, pepper and sugar to taste. Also add some water so our salsa isn't so dense.

recipe-salmon-fillet-with-sauteed-spinac.name: Recipe: Salmon Fillet with Sautéed Spinach
recipe-salmon-fillet-with-sauteed-spinac.original_name: Receta fácil: Filete de Salmón con Espinacas Salteadas
instructions: Wash and dry salmon steaks with absorbing paper. Saxony both sides with salt and pepper to taste.
Warm a large frying pan with medium-high heat and add a tablespoon of olive oil. Put salmon steaks in the frying pan with skin down and cook for 4-5 minutes till skin crisps.
Fold salmon steaks with a scatula and cook for another 3-4 minutes till fully cooked. Expand half lemon juice onto steaks while cooking.
While the salmon is cooked, it heats another frying pan with a tablespoon of olive oil. Add the minced garlic and cook them briefly until they are fragant.
Add fresh spinach to frying pan and stir them constantly for about 2 to 3 minutes till they are moved.
Remove salmon from the frying pan and serve every steak on a bed of pitted spinach. Ready to enjoy!

red-beet-eggs-for-a-crowd.name: Red Beet Eggs For A Crowd
instructions: Drain juice from canned beets into a large container with a lid; stir in apple cider vinegar, sugar, and water until sugar has dissolved.
Mix sliced beets, hard-cooked eggs, and sweet onion into the liquid; add more water so liquid covers eggs completely if needed.
Cover container and refrigerate for 1 to 3 days, stirring the eggs and beets twice daily. The longer they soak, the deeper the eggs' color will be.

red-curry-paste.name: Red Curry Paste

ingredients.prep: trimmed, tough outer layers removed, thinly sliced crosswise
torn or chopped into small pieces

ingredients.item: Cilantro roots or 3 tbsp. thinly sliced thick cilantro stems
" piece dried kaffir lime zest or 2 very thinly sliced kaffir lime leaves

Prik haeng chiles can be found at most asian markets and will often be labeled as dried thai chiles or just dried chiles. prik chee fah chiles are much harder to source; the next best equivalent is mexican puya chiles.

instructions: Pound peppercorns and coriander seeds in mortar and pestle until finely ground (if using a food processor, you will want to grind these spices in a spice mill first). Add lemongrass and galangal and pound until ground to a coarse paste, 6–8 minutes. Add chiles (if you want, soak them in hot water 10–20 minutes beforehand to soften and make them easier to break up) and pound until pasty, 6–8 minutes. Add shallots, garlic, cilantro roots, and kaffir lime zest, if using, and pound until everything comes together in a relatively smooth paste, about 5 minutes. Mix in shrimp paste; taste and add more if needed.
Do Ahead: Curry pastes are best consumed the day they are made, but you can freeze any leftovers up to 1 month.

red-tomato-pickle-recipe-lezzet.name: Red Tomato Pickle Recipe

ingredients.item: Instead of medium-sized butter or curvy beans can be used.

You can use apple vinegar, grape vinegar or delicate sour instead of white vinegar.
For a lighter aroma, you can reduce the vinegar slightly.
Fresh mint, thyme or parsley stems can be used instead of Defne leaf.
You can add bitter pepper or grainy instead of tane pepper.

rhubarb-lovers-pancakes.name: Rhubarb Lover's Pancakes
instructions: Place rhubarb in a saucepan with enough water to cover; bring to a boil. Reduce heat to medium, cover, and cook until tender, about 10 minutes. Off heat, stir in ⅓ cup sugar; set aside to cool to room temperature, about 30 minutes.

Whisk flour, baking powder, 1 teaspoon sugar, and salt together in a large bowl. Whisk milk, melted butter, egg, and vanilla extract together in a cup; stir into flour mixture until forms a smooth batter. Stir 1 cup cooled rhubarb mixture into batter.

Heat a lightly oiled griddle over medium-high heat. Drop batter by ⅓ cupfuls onto griddle; cook until bubbles form and edges are dry, 3 to 4 minutes. Flip pancakes; cook until browned on other side, 2 to 3 minutes. Repeat with remaining batter.
Serve pancakes with remaining rhubarb sauce.

rice-and-beef-jerky-bowl.name: Rice And Beef Jerky Bowl

ingredients.prep: beef jerky, cooked greens (such as kale), sliced radishes, chopped, toasted almonds, parsley leaves, and tarragon leaves (for serving)

instructions: Whisk shallot, vinegar, and oil in a small bowl; season with salt and pepper. Cover and chill until ready to use.
Place rice in a medium bowl. Arrange jerky, greens, and radish over rice. Drizzle with vinaigrette. Top with shallots, almonds, parsley, and tarragon.
Do Ahead: Shallot vinaigrette can be made 1 day ahead. Cover and chill.

ritas-self-rising-cornbread-mix.name: Rita's Self-rising Cornbread Mix
instructions: Mix cornmeal, flour, milk powder, baking powder, salt together in a bowl.

roast-beef-with-mushroom-and-red-wine-sa.name: Roast Beef With Mushroom And Red Wine Sauce
roast-beef-with-mushroom-and-red-wine-sa.original_name: Rosbife com molho de cogumelos e vinho tinto
instructions: Bar the meat with butter and season it with salt and pepper. Place the rosemary stems in the center of a tray, dispose of the meat on top and put it in the oven, preheated at 180oC for about 20 minutes.
Reduce the temperature to 110oC and let cook for another 20 minutes. Remove, wrap it in foil and let it rest.

Leve ao lume uma frigideira com azeite, deixe aquecer e junte os alhos inteiros, com casca e ligeiramente esmagados. Adicione os cogumelos e salteie-os, durante 5 minutos, em lume forte, mexendo de vez em quando.

Tempere com sal e pimenta, regue com o vinho e deixe apurar. Rejeite os alhos, adicione as natas e cozinhe durante mais 5 minutos. Retire do lume, polvilhe com salsa picada e misture. Corte o rosbife em fatias e sirva com o molho de cogumelos.

roasted-chicken-thighs-with-lemon-and-or.name: Roasted Chicken Thighs With Lemon And Oregano

ingredients.item: Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper

instructions: Preheat oven to 425°. Very thinly slice half of lemon; discard any seeds. Cut remaining lemon half into 2 wedges. Season chicken thighs with salt and pepper.

Coat a large room-temperature skillet with 1 teaspoon oil. Add chicken, skin side down. Place skillet over medium heat and cook, letting skin render and brown, and pouring off excess fat to maintain a thin coating in pan, until chicken is cooked halfway through, about 10 minutes.

Scatter half of lemon slices over chicken and half on bottom of skillet (the slices on top of the chicken will soften; those in the skillet will caramelize). Transfer skillet to oven, leaving chicken skin side down. Roast until chicken is cooked through, skin is crisp, and lemon slices on bottom of skillet are caramelized, 6-8 minutes.

Transfer chicken pieces, skin side up, and caramelized lemon slices from bottom of skillet to a warm platter. (Leave softened lemon slices in the skillet.) Return skillet to medium heat. Add oregano sprigs, shallot, garlic, and red pepper flakes; cook, stirring frequently, until fragrant, about 1 minute.

Remove skillet from heat. Add wine; cook over medium heat until reduced by half, 1-2 minutes. Add broth; cook until thickened, about 3 minutes. Squeeze 1 lemon wedge over and season sauce with salt, pepper, and juice from remaining lemon wedge, if desired. Drizzle with 2 teaspoons oil. Return chicken to skillet, skin side up, to rewarm. Serve topped with caramelized lemon slices.

roasted-swiss-chard-with-feta.name: Roasted Swiss Chard With Feta

ingredients.item: Rainbow chard - leaves and stems separated and chopped
Salt and black pepper to taste

instructions: Preheat the oven to 177°C (175 degrees C). Grease a baking sheet with olive oil.
Toss chard stems and onions with 1 tablespoon oil in a bowl; season with salt and black pepper. Transfer to the prepared baking sheet.

Bake in the preheated oven until chard stems softened and onion starting to brown on corners, about 15 minutes. Toss chard leaves with remaining 2 tablespoons oil; season with salt and black pepper. Place leaves over stem mixture; scatter feta on top.
Continue baking until stems tender, leaves begin to crisp, and feta melted and golden, about 20 minutes.

roasted-veal-with-port-wine-teleculinari.name: Roasted Veal With Port Wine
roasted-veal-with-port-wine-teleculinari.original_name: Vitela no forno com vinho do Porto
instructions: In a bowl, mix the butter, previously softened, with a little salt and pepper, until it forms a paste.
Bar the meat with the previous paste and dispose of it in an oven tray. Lay the port wine around the meat, cover with aluminium foil and put it in the oven, preheated to 180°C for about 30 minutes.
After the time indicated, discard the foil and let it cook for a few more minutes, or until the meat is at the desired point. Serve the meat sliced, along with the sauce, and follow with baked chips.

roasted-veg-with-nutritional-yeast.name: Roasted Veg With Nutritional Yeast

ingredients.prep: cut into florets with some stalk attached, or 1 medium acorn squash, seeds removed, cut into  0.5-inch slices

instructions: Preheat oven to 425°. Toss vegetables with oil on a rimmed baking sheet to coat; season with salt and pepper. Roast until deep golden brown and tender, 20–25 minutes. Let cool slightly, then toss with nutritional yeast.
Do Ahead: Veggies can be made 5 days ahead. Cover and chill.

roasted-veggie-skewers.name: Roasted Veggie Skewers
instructions: Preheat grill to medium heat.
Combine the oil, lemon juice, oregano, salt, and pepper, stirring until mixed.
Slide a zucchini slice down the skewer, followed by pieces of mushroom, red onion, and peppers, until there is a 1-inch gap at the top of the skewer. Brush each skewer liberally with the oil mixture.
Repeat with the remaining skewers and vegetables.
Grill the skewers for 12-18 minutes with the lid closed, flipping halfway.
Enjoy!

rock-shrimp-tempura-pan-fried.name: Rock Shrimp Tempura (pan-fried)
instructions: To make the dipping sauce: Whisk egg yolk, chili-garlic sauce, rice vinegar, sea salt, and black pepper together in a bowl. Pour in 3 tablespoons oil slowly, whisking constantly, until sauce is creamy.
To make the batter: Whisk ice water, flour, and egg yolk together in a large bowl to make batter.

Heat 1 tablespoon olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Dip some shrimp in batter, letting excess drip off, and cook in hot oil until browned on both sides, 3 to 5 minutes. Drain on paper towels. Repeat with remaining olive oil and shrimp.
Place cooked shrimp in a large bowl. Pour sauce on top and mix until thoroughly coated.

rustic-pork-rillette.name: Rustic Pork Rillette
instructions: Preheat oven to 275°. Place pork shoulder and belly and skin in a large saucepan. Tuck in thyme and bay leaves and add ½ cup water. Cover and braise, stirring occasionally, until meat is falling-apart tender and fat is soft, 2 ½–3 hours. Remove from oven, pluck out thyme and bay leaves and let meat rest until cool enough to handle.
Using a slotted spoon, transfer meat and skin to a large bowl; pour fat and any cooking liquid in pot into a heatproof measuring cup (you should have about 1 cup).

Shred meat with a potato masher; do not hold back. Pour in ¾ cup fat and mash meat a little bit more—mixture should look and feel almost pasty (but in a good way). Season with salt, nutmeg, and a splash of lemon juice, which will temper the richness of the meat. Taste and adjust as necessary (err on the side of too salty—it will mellow as it cools).
Pack rillette mixture into jars, pushing out any air bubbles. Top each with a few spoonfuls of reserved fat and chill until set, at least 2 hours.
Serve with bread or crackers.
Do ahead: Rillette can be made 1 week ahead; cover and chill. Let sit at room temperature 2 hours before serving.
